questions.text: How long have you been in your current role or professional situation?

options.label: Less than 2 years
2–5 years
5–10 years
10–20 years
More than 20 years

questions.text: Which best describes the trajectory of your career over the past 3 years?

options.label: Actively advancing — new responsibilities, promotions, growth
Stable — same level, similar work, no major change
Declining — role reduced, org shrinking, or market contracting
Disrupted — major change imposed on me (redundancy, restructure, relocation)
In transition — I've recently changed or am actively changing

questions.text: How long since you last felt your career was genuinely moving forward?

options.label: Within the past year
1–2 years ago
2–4 years ago
More than 4 years ago
I'm not sure it ever did

questions.text: Which best describes your current employer or work context?

options.label: Large corporation (500+ employees)
Mid-size company (50–500)
Small company or startup (<50)
Public sector or institution
Self-employed / freelance
Currently between roles

questions.text: On a typical working day, how much energy do you have left at the end of it?
How has your energy for work changed over the past 2 years?

options.label: Significantly increased
Roughly the same
Gradually declining
Sharply declined

questions.text: When you think about your work, which feeling is most frequent?

options.label: Engaged and interested
Competent but indifferent
Frustrated and blocked
Exhausted and empty
Conflicted — good at it but wrong for me
Anxious — external pressure or instability

questions.text: How often do you think about making a significant change?

options.label: Rarely — I'm broadly satisfied
Occasionally — it crosses my mind
Regularly — it's a background consideration
Constantly — it's hard to focus on anything else
I've already decided — I'm in the process of changing

questions.text: Rank these frustrations from most to least relevant to your situation right now.

options.label: I can't advance or grow where I am
I don't believe in what my work produces
I had a creative or personal path I never took
I have no time or space for anything that matters to me
I don't control enough of my own work
I'm not sure what I actually want

questions.scoring_note: Top 2 ranked options receive full weight. 3rd receives 0.5 weight. 4th–6th receive 0.

questions.text: If you imagine a version of yourself 5 years from now that you'd respect — what's most different about their work life?

options.label: They work for themselves
Their work produces something they believe in
They've finally pursued the thing they set aside
They've changed field or profession entirely
They have more control over their time
They know clearly who they are professionally
Not much is different — I need adjustment, not reinvention

questions.text: Is there something specific you've wanted to pursue professionally but haven't?

options.label: Yes — a creative or artistic practice
Yes — my own business or independent practice
Yes — a completely different profession or field
Yes — something vague I haven't fully defined
No — I know what I want, I just need the right conditions
No — I'm not sure there is such a thing for me

questions.text: What percentage of your sense of identity comes from your professional role?
When you describe yourself to someone you've just met, what leads?

options.label: My job title or employer
My field or profession
Something outside work — family, interests, projects
I find this question genuinely difficult

questions.text: Have you significantly changed professional direction before?

options.label: No — I've been in the same field since the start
Once — it went reasonably well
Once — it was difficult
Multiple times — I'm comfortable with change
I've tried but it didn't take

questions.text: Which statement most accurately describes your relationship to your current work?

options.label: It's genuinely what I'd choose if I could choose anything
It suits me reasonably well but isn't my ideal
It was once right for me but no longer is
It was never really right — I chose it for practical reasons
I'm not sure what would be right — that's part of the problem

questions.text: Is there a creative, artistic, or personal practice you've set aside over the course of your career?

options.label: Yes — and I think about it often
Yes — but I've mostly made peace with it
Possibly — I'm not sure it counts as a real loss
No — creativity isn't a significant part of my identity

questions.text: Which of the following genuinely constrain your options? Select all that apply.

options.label: Mortgage or significant financial obligations
Children at home who depend on my income
Caring responsibilities for a parent or family member
Partner's career limits my geographic options
Health issues — mine or a dependent's
Limited savings or no financial runway
Geographic location limits my options
None of the above significantly constrain me

questions.scoring_note: Sum weights from all selected options, cap total contribution per dimension at 1.0

questions.text: If you made a significant career change, roughly how many months of financial runway do you have?

options.label: Less than 3 months
3–6 months
6–12 months
12–24 months
More than 24 months
I haven't calculated this

questions.text: Have you relocated in the past 5 years for work, a partner's career, or lifestyle?

options.label: Yes — and I'm still rebuilding my professional network
Yes — and I've largely rebuilt
No — I've been in the same location
I'm considering relocating

questions.text: How specialized is your current expertise?

options.label: Very narrow — deep expert in a specific domain
Moderately specialized — clear field, some transferability
Broadly skilled — generalist with varied experience
Specialized but in a domain I want to leave entirely

questions.text: How urgent does making a change feel?

options.label: Not urgent — I'm exploring ideas
Moderate — within the next 1–2 years
Significant — within the next 6–12 months
Very urgent — I need to act soon
No urgency — I'm not sure I want to change

questions.text: Which type of change feels most honest about what you actually need?

options.label: Adjustment — same field, different role or org
Pivot — adjacent field, transfer existing skills
Reinvention — new domain, new identity, start over
Independence — same work, different structure (freelance, consulting)
I don't know yet

questions.text: When you imagine making a significant professional change, what is your dominant feeling?

options.label: Excitement — I'm ready
Cautious optimism — I want it but I'm careful
Fear — the risks feel very real
Relief — like I've already decided and just need to act
Paralysis — I can't make myself start

questions.text: How do you typically respond when you imagine a worst-case scenario of a career change going badly?

options.label: I'm reasonably comfortable with it — I'd recover
Uncomfortable but manageable with planning
It genuinely scares me and slows me down
The financial consequences would be very serious

questions.text: Have you been actively planning a change for more than a year without acting?

options.label: Yes — for 1–2 years
Yes — for more than 2 years
No — I've been thinking about it but not actively planning
No — I'm in the middle of acting
No — I'm not yet sure I want to change

questions.text: Does your work require you to say or do things you privately disagree with?

options.label: Rarely or never
Occasionally — minor compromises
Regularly — I've adapted but it costs me something
Constantly — it's become a significant source of conflict

questions.text: After a major professional achievement or goal completion, how do you typically feel?

options.label: Energized and ready for the next thing
Satisfied, then ready to move on
Strangely flat or empty
Uncertain what comes next
I haven't had a major achievement recently

questions.text: Which statement most honestly describes why you haven't changed yet?

options.label: Financial risk — I can't afford for it to go wrong
I don't know what to change to
Responsibilities — other people depend on me
Fear of failure or being exposed
I keep moving the target — the conditions are never right
I've already started changing
I'm not sure I want to — the status quo is fine

scoring.description: Each answer contributes dimension scores between 0 and 1. Final dimension score = sum of all contributing weights / max possible for that dimension, normalized to 0–1. Archetype match = cosine similarity between user dimension vector and archetype signature vector.
scoring.match_reason_logic: For each dimension where user score > 0.6 AND archetype signature > 0.6, generate a match reason string. Include top 3 matching dimensions in the result reveal.
scoring.mvp_note: In MVP, use rule-based threshold matching rather than cosine similarity. Flag any dimension > 0.7 as 'signal present'. Count archetype signatures where 3+ high-signal dimensions align. Primary = highest count. Secondary = second highest if within 1 dimension count of primary.
